channel.title: Stars & Posts — Kylin Miao
channel.description: Daily curated GitHub starred repos, Bluesky/X posts, YouTube videos and blog articles from AI leaders with AI-powered summaries.

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-29
item.description: GitHub Repos: * EnnengYang/Awesome-Model-Merging-Methods-Theories-Applications — Model Merging in LLMs, MLLMs, and Beyond: Methods, Theories, Applications and Opportunities. ACM Computing Surveys, 2026.

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-28
item.description:
Bluesky Posts: * Mark Riedl: You’ve systemed your last down * Ethan Mollick: A good example of one of the many, many small ways in which the "good old days" were terrible:
In the 1880s, the average US rural housewife had to ha... * Ethan Mollick: The old BBC "Industrial Revelations" show is a great overview of what makes an Industrial Revolution
Steam power alone wasn't enough, revolution requ... * Mark Riedl: Book 2: “The Captain’s Daughter” really raises the stakes. It’s really hard to fix things when [redacted] and then [redacted] after 900 years of trave... * Nathan Lambert: With everything going on, it gives me hope that there's such a diversity of companies building open models today.
A lot of the story of open models ... * Ethan Mollick: In my experience, all AI model routers underestimate the difficulty of non-math/coding tasks and assign them too little intelligence. This is worth ad... * Emily M. Bender: Join us tomorrow! * Emily M. Bender: Join us tomorrow! * Naomi Saphra: If you're heading to #ICML2026, you should definitely go meet my collaborators and check out our work! Highlight 🧵 for 2 main track papers, 2 positio...
Blog Articles: * Latest open artifacts (#22): Zyphra, Cohere, and Poolside are expanding the breadth of the ecosystem — Nathan Lambert: An assessment of the open ecosystem and the motivations behind releasing models

Or their p... * Ethan Mollick: A thing I am noticing is the number of folks who believe AI is “real” is larger, but now there is a growing division between people who have seen that... * Naomi Saphra: the fact that AI judges prefer sloppy AI writing makes the total death of good human-readable prose almost inevitable in scientific publishing and wri...

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-26
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * jd-opensource/JoyAI-Image — JoyAI-Image is the unified multimodal foundation model for image understanding, text-to-image generation, and instruction-guided image editing. [Python]
Bluesky Posts: * Mark Riedl: No resolution on Fable 5 yet but some US institutions and the us government can use Mythos * Mark Riedl: I would love to tell you that I enjoyed reading “There is no Antimemetics Division” but there is no such book * Marc Lanctot: Hey @radiohead.wastehq.social, after Hamlet can you do Fight Club next? 😅🙏 * Nathan Lambert: There's a lot of sloppy thinking around open models. You can ban them and make it impossible for US companies to use them, but this won't stop A) gl... * Ethan Mollick: Finally, AI finds its ultimate use case.
A diffusion model trained on burger recipes "discovers the classic Big Mac without explicit supervision and ... * Ethan Mollick: Great experiment testing how good AIs are getting at very ambitious end-to-end coding tasks. Opus 4.7, in 14 hours, was able to build a software packa... * Ethan Mollick: If you want to read an interesting AI thinking trace try "I want you to suggest two poems that you think apply very well to the current state of GenAI... * Emily M. Bender: Handing off emergency medical services to the bullshit generator? What could go wrong!? On the next Mystery AI Hype Theater 3000 livestream, join me a... * Emily M. Bender: For my academic friends, this is a really enjoyable podcast. The hosts ask excellent questions and the guests provide really open answers about how th... * Emily M. item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-25
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * alejandro-ao/tau [Python] * RhysSullivan/executor — The missing integration layer for AI agents. Let them call any OpenAPI / MCP / GraphQL / custom js functions in secure environment. [TypeScript]
Bluesky Posts: * Mark Riedl: Today's important question unresolved from a meeting earlier today: could Jean-Luc Picard pick up Mjölnir? * Ethan Mollick: It is weird that there is still a substantial set of people who believe "AI is mostly hype" at this stage: Five Eyes is warning about AI, exponential ... * Naomi Saphra: When I started my PhD I thought it was fine for images but wouldn’t work for discrete structures data like language 😂 * Nathan Lambert: The goal with my rlhf book is to make the "home on the internet" for the next generation learning post-training. That's why I'm doing all formats (lec... * Ethan Mollick: This is a fascinating and important set of data which shows us where things are going, using OpenAI as a canary in the coal mine.
The chatbot era is ... * Emily M. Bender: Achievement unlocked: Over on mansplainers central (aka Mastodon) someone replied to this post by telling me that I should have suggested "stochastic ... * Emily M. Bender: Are you annoyed with the anthropomorphizing language being used in the "AI" discourse, but not sure how to talk about this stuff without it? @nannaini... * Emily M. Bender: The @uwnews.uw.edu daily digest email this morning is a study in AI hype
🧵>> * Emily M. Bender: The term "AI" muddies the discourse by lumping together disparate technologies and overselling each of them, to boot.
Here is Exhibit #15698
Source:... * Emily M. Bender: "AI" is going to make medical care more efficient., they say. How's that going? Just about as you'd predict...
www.seattletimes.com/business/med... * Emily M. Bender: Also available as video on PeerTube: peertube.dair-institute.org/w/pqW5eyyUp1... * Emily M. Bender: “‘AI’ might not be good for xyz, but you can’t deny that it’s helpful for programming” -- sound familiar? In Mystery AI Hype Theater 3000, Ep 79 @alex... * Naomi Saphra: I'm so happy when other people write papers on nondeterministic factors in training. embrace the chaos * angela zhou: Check out our #FAccT2026 tutorial tomorrow on Bridging Predictions and Interventions in Social Systems! We're going to be building a predictions - int...

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-24
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * mdn/mcp — MDN's prototype MCP server [JavaScript] * krea-ai/krea-2 — Official inference code for Krea 2 [Python] Bluesky Posts: * Mark Riedl: I'm going to use this in a lecture on chain-of-thought for my NLP class * Naomi Saphra: People don’t talk about how great it is to be monolingual when you listen to great music with really stupid lyrics * Mark Riedl: It was a dark night. A night like no other. Even the streetlights seemed to want to hold their melancholy glow to themselves. That’s when trouble walk... * Mark Riedl: The plot thickens. Mythos identified vulnerabilities in NSA systems but did not exploit them. Reporting on this was overblown, which led to a cascade ... * Marc Lanctot: Goofy's Sky School is an awesome little coaster at Disney's California Adventure park @disneyparks.bsky.social We arrived early two days and rode it... * Marc Lanctot: Back in Anaheim again for @vidcon.bsky.social ▶️ Pleasantly surprised to see that @markhamillofficial.bsky.social will be talking at #vidcon 🤩✨️🌌 !... * Margaret Mitchell: 🧊 PSA: if you’re in a heat wave, consider putting ice out in bowls/bird baths for the birds. A lot of them will die otherwise. 😔 🪶 * Nathan Lambert: "Knowledge wants to be free" is an unofficial motto for Interconnects and related projects like my book :) rlhfbook.com/course * Nathan Lambert: Another quick lecture -- I've been asked many times for prereq's to my book and what you should know, so built a little lecture (with GLM 5.2) to cove... * hardmaru: Excited to partner with OpenRouter ⚡ Products like OpenRouter Fusion and Sakana Fugu have sparked a serious conversation about dependency and resilie... * Ethan Mollick: The ability of Codex (and Code) to solve problems on my various Windows machines has saved me so much effort. Just one of the most annoying uses of ti... * Emily M. Bender: This is horrible. * Naomi Saphra: before a model's optimizer settles into a smooth basin, gradient updates can explore in seemingly any direction, taking nearly orthogonal steps. This ... * angela zhou: Kwak says after Accenture tried to get enterprises to adopt AI as quickly as possible, AI has reached scale in most areas in both Accenture and its cl... * angela zhou: initially i felt a bit guilty about traveling / linking together a few things, but the boyle heights warehouse has been on fire for a week, so not a b... Blog Articles: * Scaling Laws, Carefully — Lilian Weng:

Scaling laws are one of the most critical empirical findings in deep learning. The observation is simple in form: the training loss $L$ decreases p...

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-22
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * maziyarpanahi/openmed — Local-first healthcare AI: clinical NER & HIPAA PII de-identification that runs 100% on-device. 1,000+ medical models, 12 languages, Apple MLX + Python, no cloud, no patient data leaving your network. Apache-2.0 [Python] * mujocolab/mjlab — Isaac Lab API, powered by MuJoCo-Warp, for RL and robotics research [Python] * pcuenca/LlamaLanguageModels — Foundation Models API for llama.cpp [Swift] Bluesky Posts: * hardmaru: Human intelligence is fundamentally a collective intelligence. We solve complex problems by participating in a vast cultural network that builds upon ... * Simon Willison: My parallel agent side-project today was having Claude Code port the new Moebius image pinpointing model to ONNX in order to run it entirely in the br... * Mark Riedl: Talks between Anthropic and the US government regarding Fable 5 release www.politico.com/news/2026/06... * Nathan Lambert: Excited to share a new open-source, RL recipe paper! TMax is the best openly available terminal-bench style training data, establishing the open front... * Nathan Lambert: GLM-5.2 is the step change for open agents. Aka GLM-5.2 is the “DeepSeek moment” for agents. We enter a new world where the top end of agentic capabil... * Gaël Varoquaux: You're only a real statisticien if you can say "Heteroscedasticity" without flinching * Yoshua Bengio: An interesting new paper by my recent PhD graduate on how AI agents' greed for visible incentives can lead them to abandon their safety alignment. Y... * Ethan Mollick: The thing that made Fable so impressive was its creative problem-solving & good judgement calls across long-running projects You can see this when I ... * Emily M. Bender: TFW the example you use as a nightmare case of bad application of language technology has actually been implement (and in the works since 2019) in you... * Ben Recht: LOL. * Ben Recht: For my Bluesky friends, here is a summary of the Twitter war between medical doctors and tech bros over the Midjourney ultrasound vaporware. Blog Articles: * Porting the Moebius 0.2B image inpainting model to run in the browser with Claude Code — Simon Willison:

This morning on Hacker News I saw Moebius: ... * GLM-5.2 is the step change for open agents — Nathan Lambert: A capability threshold I've been carefully monitoring.

GitHub Repos: * Snowflake-AI-Research/Arctic-Platform — Arctic Training and Inference Platform [Python] * Choochmeque/tauri-webdriver — Cross-platform WebDriver server for Tauri applications (Linux, macOS, Windows) [Rust] * Coding-Solo/godot-mcp — MCP server for interfacing with Godot game engine. Provides tools for launching the editor, running projects, and capturing debug output. [JavaScript]
Bluesky Posts: * Simon Willison: Just launched Datasette Apps - a plugin for Datasette that lets you host full HTML+JS apps in an iframe sandbox that can query your database and do in... * Mark Riedl: Grok was used in operation Epic Fury to enable the deployment of over 2,000 munitions to 2,000 separate targets. This was revealed as national defense... * Mark Riedl: Finally, some good news * Mark Riedl: I'm writing a research proposal in which I describe my work in terms of "recursive self improvement". God help me. * Mark Riedl: Everyone should read this, just to see how they implemented a perceptron (a very basic neural network) in Age of Empires II arxiv.org/abs/2605.31514
... * Mark Riedl: I miss the future that we never got * Marc Lanctot: Haha, this is neat! 😁
Gemini knows me best 😍🙏✨️ * Nathan Lambert: Banning Open Source AI Would Be A Mistake A general audience PSA with Kevin Xu of Interconnected, while AI regulation is in the air in DC www.intercon... * Yoshua Bengio: C'est un grand honneur d’avoir reçu cette semaine l'insigne d'officier de l'Ordre national du Québec. Il reste beaucoup de chemin à parcourir pour s’... * Ethan Mollick: More evidence, from a large-scale study in China, that using AI hurts learning if it undermines mental effort. When homework time drops due to AI use,... * Ethan Mollick: Some (early) evidence that managers have the highest success rate in using Claude Code for coding.
I have been arguing that management is an AI super... * Emily M. Bender: Hey German speaking friends: Do you have a name for the feeling associated with watching a mutual on social who's a mutual because of historical share... * Naomi Saphra: Random variation in training and generation legit is the most underrated thing
Blog Articles: * Banning Open Source AI Would Be A Mistake — Nathan Lambert: This post was originally an op-ed co-authored with Kevin Xu of Interconnected for a general, non-technical audience.

They have a very good flash model, but a very good flash model can't do frontier work ... * Emily M. Bender: Co-signed. Also, every time you see that framing, note how it zeroes in on what the specific "AI" system is allegedly doing and takes a similarly nar... * Emily M. This is one of my favorite conferences. And you get extra time. * Mark Riedl: 🧵 * Mark Riedl: I just asked Gemini a question through the Google website in AI mode and it did its usual search and then gave me the search results as a list of webs... * Marc Lanctot: $240k ??? 😱 These competitions are getting 🤑🤑🤑 Ka-ching! * Marc Lanctot: Third @bsky.app release in 10 days. 🤩🦋😎 * Nathan Lambert: I wrote a little state of the union on the state of Interconnects, how I ended up with a loyal niche audience, and how I’m tweaking operations to ensu... * Yoshua Bengio: Je suis ravi d’être à Québec aujourd'hui pour recevoir mon insigne d’officier de l'Ordre national du Québec, accompagné de Daniel Jutras, recteur de l... * Ethan Mollick: I have a fun, oddly useful AI benchmark: "build me a procedurally generated 3D simulation showing the evolution of a harbor town from 3000 BC to 3000 ... * Ethan Mollick: If the leaked financial data is right, OpenAI is profitable on serving customers with 40%+ gross margins. But training remains incredibly expensive. ... * Emily M. Bender: The last thing we need is the US government having an even greater interest in the success of these grifters. * Ben Recht: Everyone strives to be a better person. So why is maxxing culture grotesque? Blog Articles: * GLM-5.2 is probably the most powerful text-only open weights LLM — Simon Willison:

Anthropic probably doesn’t want to guess wrong nor incur more ire, but my reading of... * Marc Lanctot: Check out GameLab: an eval benchmark for language models playing games!
Initial results on Gin rummy, Crosswords, and Block Champ!
Backed by the gam... * Nathan Lambert: It's hard to pinpoint open-closed gap and so-on, but I trust the Arena team and just look where GLM 5.2 is on this. An MIT licensed, to be open weight... * Nathan Lambert: New podcast with my (former) colleague Finbarr Timbers on the state of frontier post-training recipes and how we got here. A fun review of how we went... * Ethan Mollick: Fable's implementation of Borges's Library of Babel, finished posthumously by Opus 4.8. Quite accurate to the story.
It is multiplayer (others appear... * Emily M. Bender: This whole thread up & down is important and insightful.
I want to add one linguistic point here:

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-15
item.description:
Bluesky Posts: * Mark Riedl: China may have accesed Fable/Mythos 5. Export-control, which was used to force Anthropic to shut down Fable, would do nothing to stop corporate espion... * Naomi Saphra: The last thing I did with Fable was generate a bunch of videos for the paper thread I’m posting as soon as arxiv posts it. * Mark Riedl: Don't overlook the importance of the "retrieval" part of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
Cornell Tech researchers find it is trivially easy to ... * Nathan Lambert: I launched 3 more videos in my post-training course! 1. Lecture 5: The rise of reasoning models 2. Lecture 6: DPO derivation, intuitions, and practice... * Ethan Mollick: Fable was really good, as I wrote last week after testing it.
It was a leap, but that may because, as exponential gains progress, the improvements in... * Ethan Mollick: Very clever experiment by Josh Tycko on models and data analysis. It matches what I would expect: models have become quite good at data methodology bu... * Ethan Mollick: It is a good time for moonshots.
AI has reached a level where there are transformative projects that could result in huge social good, but require pu... * Naomi Saphra: We don’t always know what problems are hard for LLMs. So devs evaluate on tasks HUMANS find hard or on broad benchmarks. What if we could instead anti... * angela zhou: i feel like if academia doesn't work out for me, maybe i can become an applied researcher at an evaluation shop and then partial identification could ...

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-14
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * charmbracelet/vhs — Your CLI home video recorder 📼 [Go] * tamnd/ascii-gif — Turn a terminal session into a good-looking animated GIF. A vhs wrapper with an attractive default look. [Go] * Stirling-Tools/Stirling-PDF — #1 PDF Application on GitHub that lets you edit PDFs on any device anywhere [TypeScript] * Emanuele-web04/synara — The best place to build with your AI sub [TypeScript] * shadcn/improve — Use your most capable model to audit your codebase and write plans for cheaper models to execute.

I haven’t been keeping up with sport * Nathan Lambert: Welcome to the AGI era of AI governance It's a one-way door and we weren't ready for it. Especially the open-source community celebrating Anthropic's ... * Ethan Mollick: Final one-shot prompt I did before the Fable interruption: "build me a cool simulation thing that lets me demo the various forms of FTL travel from bo... * Emily M. It made a beautiful little art game,... * angela zhou: Ugh www.nytimes.com/2026/06/11/u... * Mark Riedl: Project Mail Harry * Mark Riedl: That is something magical about university campuses during summer * Mark Riedl: “policing minister… lauded the transformational potential of the technology.”

But the case studies turned out to be AI hallucinations. www.ft.c... * Ethan Mollick: 10 months later, I gave Claude Code with Fable the same brief, asking it to construct SimRefinery from surviving screenshots and documentation. Fully ... * Ethan Mollick: The shape of the graph is getting very familiar. * Ethan Mollick: There has been a push to use OpenEvidence AI for doctors. But this paper suggests general models are much better: “Frontier LLMs outperformed clinical... * Emily M. Bender: If every billionaire is a policy failure, a trillionaire is 1000 policy failures in a trenchcoat. * Emily M. item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-10
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * QwenLM/qwen-code — An open-source AI coding agent that lives in your terminal. [TypeScript]
Bluesky Posts: * Simon Willison: Wrote up my initial impressions of Claude Fable 5 - it has a big model smell: slow, expensive and capable of crunching through pretty much everything ... * Ethan Mollick: Science fiction authors in the order you want them to be right about AI: Iain Banks Becky Chambers Martha Wells Douglas Adams Charles Stross (Singular... * Ethan Mollick: One thing I mentioned in passing in my Fable post is that, for long running tasks, Fable starts to develop its own dialect as its many agents and task... * Ethan Mollick: The fact that Anthropic may take away subscription access to Fable in two weeks is weird & discourages investing in learning about the model.
Subscri... * Mark Riedl: Is “you deserve better than that” a Claude-ism? It sounds like a Claude-ism. * Mark Riedl: You can only take it into the theater if you didn't want popcorn tho * Mark Riedl: Is something “too dangerous” if it’s easy to slap on guardrails?
Tigers are really dangerous.
Tigers are at the zoo in my town. Don’t go in the ti... * Margaret Mitchell: www.newscientist.com/article/2529... * Ethan Mollick: Fable: "write me a rhyming poem with six four line stanzas, each stanza removes another vowel. the first has no u, the second no u or i, etc." * Emily M. Bender: 1. I am not an AI researcher 2. "Doesn't just repeat back to you what you said" ... isn't what any chatbots do, nor what that metaphor was about. 3. F... * Emily M. Bender: Also available as video on PeerTube:
peertube.dair-institute.org/w/jV5u1DVL4b... * Emily M. Bender: Why Bernie why?? @nathaliemarechal.net joins me and @alexhanna.bsky.social to observe and contextualize Sen Sanders’ descent into AI Doomerism:
www.b... * Naomi Saphra: Registration is open! * Naomi Saphra: Claude users checking whether the fast models are overtuned today like sailor testing the wind with their fingers
YouTube Videos: * Growing on Purpose: The Work That Makes You. Jeremy Howard on human flourishing in the time of AI. (Jeremy Howard)

GitHub Repos: * TeichAI/teich [Python] Bluesky Posts: * Ethan Mollick: Both Anthropic and OpenAI mention the possibilities of slowing AI development in their latest "what comes next" in AI posts, but say they need to be a... * Simon Willison: A TIL on using agentsview.io to calculate token spending with Claude Fable 5 despite that model not yet being included in the AgentsView pricing datab... * Mark Riedl: Dude, I just wanted to Google the Am (Automated Mathematician) system because I wanted to know when it was invented. Is this so unreasonable? * Mark Riedl: Lawyers on both sides of this case either admitted to directly using AI or admitted to rubber stamping legal briefs that had been prepared with AI wit... * Mark Riedl: April: Claude Mythos was too dangerous for release. 40 organizations got early access to fix their software security issues. June: Claude Mythos rele... * Margaret Mitchell: Come join us to socialize, virtually! * Nathan Lambert: Why I think Anthropic's uneven safety policies with the release of Claude Fable 5 undermine the broader AI community's cohesion and accelerate us to m... * Ethan Mollick: I've had access to Claude 5 Fable for a bit. A genuine jump in capability, I could feed it a 15 page design document for a project and it would work f... * Ethan Mollick: The New York Times published a roundtable discussion among Daron Acemoglu, Dean Ball, Clara Shih & myself about the future of AI & who wins at work. I... * Naomi Saphra: ✨ it's coming ✨ NEMI 2026 will be lit. It will also be the new BU interp supergroup's debut ball. Come meet us! * Ben Recht: Have to appreciate the audacity of Anthropic defending three successive model releases with diminishing quality by claiming it's both intentional and ... I didn't have early access to today's Claude Fable 5 release, but I've spent t... * Claude Fable 5 and new AI safety fables — Nathan Lambert: One step further into the power politics of frontier AI systems.

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-01
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * LiangSu8899/FlashRT — FlashRT is a high-performance realtime inference engine for small-batch, latency-sensitive AI workloads. The flagship integration is production VLA control for Pi0, Pi0.5, GROOT N1.6, and Pi0-FAST. Also support llm e.g, qwen3.6-27B [C++] * neovim/neovim — Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability [Vim Script] * hyperpuncher/chough — "it just vibes your audio into text bro" - 🐦‍⬛ [Go] * ggml-org/llama.pages — llama.cpp's official website [Svelte] * huggingface/OpenEnv — An interface library for RL post training with environments. [Python] * coyotte508/publish-to-hf — Test publish to HF * google-gemma/gemma-skills — Skills for the Gemma and model/agent interactions [Python]

Includes citations to relevant academic work.... * Nathan Lambert: Can closed labs can create margins with very high cost services in the long-term? I say yes, but only in a few niches like high end knowledge work. Me... * Emily M. Bender: Don't illustrate posts about The AI Con or any of my work with slop (aka synthetic images) challenge: Level impossible, it seems alas.
